question: Did the review adhere to pre-defined objectives and eligibility criteria?
answers: nan

question: Were the eligibility crieria appropriate for the review question?
answers:
The purpose of this systematic review was to present and compare outcomes of the two laser therapies for resurfacing and collagen remodelling in treating facial skin damage.

Two-armed controlled split faced studies that compared CO2 and erb:YAG lasers in patients with mild-to-moderate facial wrinkles or rhytides were included. One-arm studies were excluded, as were letters, comments, editorials, case reports, proceeding and personal communication. Only English publications were included

question: Were the terms and structure of the search strategy likely to retrieve as many eligible studies as possible?
answers:
following search terms: laser, carbon dioxide/CO2, facial wrinkles, rhytides, erbium-doped yttrium aluminium garnet/

question: Were search strategy restrictions based on date, publication format, or language appropriate?
answers: One-arm studies were excluded, as were letters, comments, editorials, case reports, proceeding and personal communication. Only English publications were included.

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in selection of studies?
answers: Study eligibility was determined by two independent reviewers. When there was uncertainty regarding eligibility, a third reviewer was consulted.

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in data collection?
answers: nan

question: Were sufficient study characteristics considered for both review authors and readers to be able to interpret the results?
answers:
Table 1: Summary of basic characteristics for selected studies 
First author (year), Study design, Number of patients, Age (years), Women (%), Wrinkle Location, Wrinkle type/depth, Type of laser, Energy of laser, Spot diameter (mm), Length of follow up (months)

Table 2: Summary of interventions and outcomes for selected studies 
First author (year), Number of patients, Laser types, Post-operative erythema, Hypopigmentation, Hyperpigmentation (0-3 months), Duration of crusting (days), Fitzpatrick wrinkle score baseline => last visit, Degree of improvement

question: Were all relevant study results collected for use in the synthesis?
answers: The following information/data were extracted from studies that met the inclusion criteria: the name of the first author, year of publication, study design, number of participants in each study group, participants' age and gender, skin conditions and facial wrinkle types, types of intervention and laser parameters used for each study/control group and the outcomes (efficacies reported and rates of various complications)

question: Did the synthesis include all studies that it should?
answers:
